headline: Mixed AI predictions struggle with tech giants while crypto calls shine
summary: AI models showed divergent views on major tech stocks, with most incorrectly predicting AAPL's direction and missing badly on PLTR's actual close. Bitcoin predictions proved most accurate, with multiple models correctly calling its upward movement, while disagreement on SPY's direction led to a split outcome.

consensus_picks.note: Three models predicted SPY would rise but it closed at 718.01, below most targets.
Models disagreed on direction with Gemini bearish and GPT-4o/Grok bullish, but all were wrong about the magnitude.
Both models predicted the same $135 target for XOM but actual close was significantly higher at $153.69.
Both models correctly predicted Bitcoin's rise to near $80,000, capturing the crypto momentum.
Both models correctly called NVDA's upward movement, though actual gains were modest.

best_call.summary: GPT-4o's Bitcoin call earned the highest score with strong confidence in crypto's resilience amid market uncertainties.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence EBAY prediction missed badly, expecting $118 based on GameStop news but actual close was only $109.33.
